notes: W8D1 Push — selective push week. Skipped Saturday KB (too sore) = extra rest = weirdly strong today. Push press 45x8x3 with pauses at top and slow controlled descent — best push press session ever, completely confidence boosting. Chest press JUMPED to 190x8 (up from 180). Incline DB at 50 with tempo (2-2-2-2). Kinesis fly still humbling at 8. Tri extension pyramided 55→65→75x12 which makes no sense but happened (nap + rest?). Straight arm pulldown yolo'd to 120. Only 15s available for lat raises but slow and controlled was good. Right shoulder still crackly on lat raises — strongest indicator of the shoulder issue.
cardio: 12 min zone 2 run to gym + 12 min zone 2 run back

sets: 45/side x8 (small hip press, pausing 2s at top, slow down)
45/side x8 (held at top for 5 seconds! slow and controlled, felt full body compression)
exercises.notes: Best push press session EVER. Parallel feet, slow and controlled up and down, 2-second pause at top, last set held for 5 seconds. Felt the entire compression through the body. Completely confidence boosting. Right shoulder still crackly but didn't limit anything. 45 with this tempo is harder and more valuable than 50 fast.

Only 15s available (gym busy). Super slow and controlled. Right shoulder definitely rough — this exercise is the strongest indicator of whatever shoulder thing is going on. Completed all sets though. Happy with the form check at this weight.
190x8!! Up from 180. Weirdly strong. Full range, slow and controlled, no collapse. Set 4 got 7 clean + 1 half. 190 is the new working weight. Skipping KB Saturday = extra rest = this.

sets: 50 x8+2 (tried 3-1 tempo, died at 8, picked up for 2 more)
exercises.notes: Tempo work: pauses up and down. Full range, slow, dying at end but pushed through. Set 2 tried 3-count up 1 down, died at 8 but picked up and did 2 more. The tempo makes 50 WAY harder than 55 was without tempo. Only got 2 sets in.

sets: 8 x12 (easier standing further back but still hard)
exercises.notes: Harder than pec deck — less structured, requires more stabilization. Standing distance affects difficulty. Still humbling at 8. 3x12 completed. Note: position relative to machine matters a lot.
Right shoulder crackly but got through all three. Good weight, stable.

WHAT. Pyramided from 55 to 75 and got 12 on everything. 75 was crazy hard but still got mostly good 12. W7: was doing 54x12. Either different cable setting, the nap, or the extra rest day unlocked something. Need to verify the number next week.
Yolo'd up to 120 from 110. Died but got 12 on first set with full ROM. 10 and 9 on sets 2-3. Crazy hard. 120 is the new challenge weight.

notes: W8D2 — Skipped KB for work dinner. Extremely sore (psoas, deep leg muscles, adductors). Recovery day: stretching, hot tub, 40 min walk to Stanford hospital cafe. Everything tight and impossible to roll out.

W8D3 Pull — came in extremely sore (psoas, quads, adductors, deep everything). Run to gym was hard. Busy gym so had to jump around stations. Despite feeling like death, pull-ups felt surprisingly easy relative to body state. DB rows at 85 were ugly but completed. Cable crunch at 120 feeling like a legit new muscle. Lat pulldowns at 160 strict again.
cardio: 12 min zone 2 run to gym + 12 min zone 2 run back

exercises.notes: Felt surprisingly easy given how destroyed the body was. Climber handle 10 good form, wide 9+1 negative. Close grip died at 7, wide again died at 5 slow ones. Total: 31 reps. Down from 36 last week but body was wrecked coming in.

sets: 50 x20 (slow strict, expected to die faster)
exercises.notes: 42.5 warm-up then 50x20x2. Slow and strict on all sets. Surprised at endurance — expected to fail earlier on the 50s. Good shoulder health work.

sets: 3 sets — dead on last 3 reps each, gave up strict form
exercises.notes: Strict form abandoned on last ~3 reps each set. Getting the work in but not pretty.
Incline bench concentration curls. Bench angle matters — 6 (more upright) felt different than 5. Last couple reps on set 3 needed support. Solid 3x10 at 25.
Did the full pyramid. 85s were definitely not strict — not even the first two. Wild that he could even pick them up given how sore everything was. Completed though.
Hard to find weights so went with 30 strict with hold. 3x15 completed but extremely hard at the end. Good isolation work — chest support removes the ability to cheat.
160 strict again! Set 3 was 8 strict + 2 more after a short pause. Considering going lighter for better form/feel — 'lighter feels better maybe?' Consistent at 160 across W7-W8.
Back left single pulley. 120x15x2. Second set was hard. Good bicep finisher.
120x12x2. Hard but could be harder — feels like a new muscle activating. Really good mind-muscle connection. Up from 110 last week. Could push to 130 next week.

notes: W8D4 — Skipped KB again. Second KB skip in a row (W8D2 was also skipped). Body still recovering from deep soreness week. Legs tomorrow.

W8D5 Legs — slow/tempo day. Good barbell press form work at 80 focusing on ROM. 45-plate back extensions BRUTAL when done slow — likely the main contributor to the week's deep soreness. New seated leg curl settings (5 m 0 1) feel correct — dropped to 90, built to 110. Hip adductor machine feels broken (305 didn't feel hard, arms couldn't move the stack) — ended up at 315 mostly for stretch value. Couldn't get leg press so did Smith machine single-leg Bulgarian-style (45/side) — excellent substitute, last set with more support-leg use gave much better ROM. Single-leg calf raises surprisingly easy at 45/side until adding a plate under the ball of foot. Lunges: 40s pair felt great for 2 sets, then something seized bilaterally in the hip flexor region on set 2 — finished last set bodyweight. Both hip flexors/TFL cranky, massaging helps.
cardio: 12 min zone 2 run to gym + 12 min zone 2 run back

exercises.notes: Pretty good push on all sets. ROM can still improve at 80 — KEEP the weight, work on form and tempo. Slow and controlled is the goal, not load.

First set was already dead — this is probably what's been making everything so sore all week. Two more sets slow and controlled, felt like burning. Not deathly hard during the sets but after all three: can barely walk. Slow/controlled IS the way on this. Back-ext at 45 is likely the hidden driver of the whole week's deep soreness.

sets: 90 x (new settings, getting used to it)
90 x (second set to lock in feel)
exercises.notes: NEW MACHINE SETTINGS: 5 m 0 1 — the old settings were wrong. With the correct settings 90 feels right, did it twice to calibrate. Third set at 110 was pretty hard. Baseline resets here — future weeks compare against these numbers, not older-setting numbers.

sets: 315 x12 + 12 pulses (last super hard, not full ROM)
exercises.notes: Machine likely BROKEN — pushed the stack all the way to 305 and it didn't feel hard; tried to move it with arms and couldn't budge it. Weight reading is unreliable. Only adductor machine in this gym — will need to re-evaluate on a different machine elsewhere to know real numbers. That said: fantastic hip/inner-thigh stretch at top. Last set slow 2-count + hold, last reps not full ROM, then 12 pulses to finish. Open question: is this actually doing anything beyond the stretch?

sets: 45/side x8 each leg (hard, not full ROM)
45/side x8 each leg (used support leg more, got way more ROM)
exercises.notes: Substitute for leg press (machine taken). Smith machine, one foot on bench behind, 45lb each side. HARD. First two sets short ROM. Set 3 figured out to use the support leg more actively — ROM opened up dramatically. Still prefers leg press but this is a fantastic alternative, especially for unilateral work. Keep as option when leg press unavailable.

sets: 45/side x20 each + plate under ball of foot (hard last few)
exercises.notes: Smith machine single-leg. Surprisingly easy at this weight — bodies are weird. Last set added a plate under the ball of the foot for deeper stretch/ROM — made it way harder, struggled on last few reps. Use the plate-under-foot variation going forward for real challenge.

sets: bodyweight x (weights were hard even to rack)
exercises.notes: Walking lunges, 40lb DBs in each hand. First 2 sets good. End of set 2 something seized bilaterally in the hip-flexor / upper-hip region — very painful. Didn't pull anything but couldn't continue loaded. Set 3 bodyweight only, and even racking the DBs afterward was hard. BOTH sides of upper hip (likely hip flexors / TFL) complaining — massaging helps a lot. Note: 40lb lunges at the end of a long legs day after fried hip flexors all week was too much. Next week: cap lunge volume or move lunges earlier in session.
